commit.message:
Add context tokens field to Local mode

Adds a persistent "Context tokens" field in the Local Settings box
(below the Model field, default 32768). At launch, the value is
injected as OLLAMA_NUM_CTX and LM_STUDIO_NUM_CTX so both Ollama and
LM Studio use the configured context window. Values below 32768 are
clamped. The setting is saved and restored across sessions.

fix: replace ambiguous was/were-ing distractors in en/02-01 and en/02-02

Blind verification found that past-continuous distractors (was -ing)
were accepted as also-correct in sentence-completion questions. Replaced
with has/have + pp or future (unambiguously wrong with past time markers),
and added explicit past-time anchors to two sentences that lacked them.

Recompiled both English past-simple and present-simple sections.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix slow note loading after fresh sign-in (#22)

Stabilize repository identity across keyring changes by reading the
keyring via a ref instead of closing over the Map.  Previously every
vault-machine phase (local keyring load, cloud key cache) produced a
new Map reference that recreated the repository, cancelling in-flight
sync and resetting note content mid-load.

Also remove the isVaultUnlocked gate on day/month clicks so a dot
click always navigates and shows "Decrypting..." while the vault is
still unlocking, instead of silently doing nothing.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(mcp): parallel bars cache, Parquet export worker, e2e tests + bug fixes

Bar pipeline improvements:
- screener backtest() uses SQLite as L2 bar cache: checks storage first (if
  fresh ≤2 days + ≥200 bars), saves to storage after Yahoo fetch
- screener run(bars=true) also persists fetched bars to storage
- bars-worker.js: background job every 6h — exports SQLite→Parquet via DuckDB,
  cleans intraday bars older than 7 days, cleans orphaned Parquet files
- storage.js: add latestDate(), countBars(), cleanOldIntraday(keepDays)
- index.js: import + start bars-worker, add export_parquet + bars_worker_status tools

Bug fixes (caught by e2e tests):
- yahoo.js: getQuotes() switched from broken v7/quote (401) to v8/chart per-symbol
  (parallel, no auth required) — fixes regime detection and screener quote fetch
- screener.js compileDSL: fix double-prefix bug (q.q.changePct) by using
  single-pass regex alternation instead of sequential field replacements
- screener.js backtest(): support comma-separated symbols and single tickers,
  not just named universe keys
- universe.js get_universe tool: add missing await

E2E test suite (37 tests, all passing):
- cache TTL, invalidate, stats
- BarsStorage: save/get/latestDate/countBars/meta/CSV/NDJSON/catalog/cleanOldIntraday
- Yahoo: getQuotes, getBars (multi-symbol)
- DSL: compileDSL simple/AND/OR/invalid, calcRSI, calcEMA, calcATR
- Screener run() with filter, impossible filter, bars=true RSI enrichment
- Backtest: summary+trades, bars persisted to storage
- Universe: list, searchTickers, fetchYahooScreener, get (StockAnalysis live), getWithMeta
- BarsWorker: status shape, runNow
- Regime: detect() returns valid regime string

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: モバイル音声入力をタップトグル方式に変更・コンテキストメニューを多層で抑止

原因:
- Push-to-Talk (pointerDown/Up) は短いタップで即 stop → 「すぐ切れる」
- Chrome Android が 600ms 以上の長押しを画像長押しと判断し
  コンテキストメニュー(コピー/Googleで検索)を表示 → pointerCancel → 認識停止

対応:
- onClick トグル方式に変更（タップ1回でON、再タップでOFF）
  長押し不要のためコンテキストメニューが根本的に発生しない
- ネイティブ contextmenu リスナー（capture フェーズ）を追加し
  React の onContextMenu が効かない Chrome Android でも確実に抑止
- touchAction: manipulation / WebkitTouchCallout: none を inline style で適用
- 絵文字を aria-hidden + pointerEvents:none の span に包み
  Chrome の「画像」認識を防いでコンテキストメニューを抑止

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: harden git tools security + code quality improvements (#150)

## Summary

- Implement 6 workspace-scoped git tools: `GitStatusTool`, `GitLogTool`,
`GitDiffTool`, `GitBranchTool`, `GitCommitTool`, `GitCloneTool`
- All tools share `_BaseGitTool` base class with workspace boundary
enforcement and common `_run_git()` subprocess helper
- Security hardening: flag injection prevention (`_check_ref`), clone
URL scheme validation (`_is_allowed_clone_url`),
`GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1`, `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0`, `shell=False`
subprocess execution, path traversal prevention
- Removed unused `GitToolError` (dead code — tools use
`ToolExecutionResult` for error reporting)
- Updated DESIGN_SPEC.md and CLAUDE.md to reflect new built-in tools

## Security measures

| Threat | Mitigation |
|--------|-----------|
| Git flag injection via ref/branch params | `_check_ref()` rejects
values starting with `-` |
| Clone URL SSRF / local file exfiltration | `_is_allowed_clone_url()`
allowlists `https://`, `http://`, `ssh://`, `git://`, SCP-like |
| Shell injection | `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` (never
`shell=True`) |
| Path traversal | `_validate_path()` with `resolve()` + `relative_to()`
|
| Config-based attacks | `GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1` blocks system git
config |
| Interactive credential prompts | `GIT_TERMINAL_PROMPT=0` |

## Test plan

- [x] 79 git tool tests (6 flag injection, 6 URL validation, workspace
validation, all tool operations)
- [x] 2133 total tests pass, 95.28% coverage
- [x] ruff lint + format clean
- [x] mypy strict clean
- [x] Pre-reviewed by 9 agents, 16 findings addressed

## Review coverage

9 agents ran in parallel: code-reviewer, python-reviewer,
pr-test-analyzer, silent-failure-hunter, type-design-analyzer,
logging-audit, resilience-audit, security-reviewer, docs-consistency. 16
findings triaged and implemented (3 Critical security, 5 Major code, 5
Major docs, 3 Medium tests).

Closes #132

---------

commit.message:
fix: preserve error state when recognition ends after an error

The onerror event fires before onend, so _setState('idle') in onend
was immediately clearing the --error class. Now the error display
is preserved through onend and cleared by its own 1.5s timer.

This fixes the issue where the mic button would briefly flash red
and return to normal without showing the error state (e.g. on Vivaldi).

commit.message:
fix: production hardening - security, stability, dead code, config, hot-path, tests (#485)

* More log output

* fix: production hardening - security, stability, dead code, config, hot-path, tests

- Security: check crypto/rand error in generateAuthToken; fatal on failure instead of
  silently producing a zero-value token
- Stability: replace panic() in OTel noLeadingZeroIDGenerator with warn log + math/rand
  fallback; add package-level test seam for injecting rand failures
- Dead code: remove entire sync protocol subsystem (RequestSync, RequestSyncFromAll,
  handleSyncRequest, handleSyncResponse, Sync*Message factories, Sync*Payload types)
  that was never wired into production; delete sync_test.go and testutil.go
- Config: add CoordinatorConfig.Validate() covering Listen, S3 size limits, MemberlistSeeds,
  AdminPeers glob patterns, and OTLPEndpoint URL; wire into coordinator init paths
- Hot-path: replace sync.RWMutex on relay/filter/localIP in Forwarder with atomic.Value
  / atomic.Pointer for lock-free reads on the per-packet forwarding path
- Tests: add JWT expiry tests (Expired, WithRelay_Expired), CoordinatorConfig.Validate
  table-driven tests, OTel fallback test, and handshake context-cancel/timeout tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review feedback on hardening changes

- forwarder: sanitise typed-nil relay at write time in SetRelay so loadRelay()
  needs no per-packet reflect.ValueOf().IsNil() call on the hot path
- config: tighten OTLPEndpoint validation to reject non-http/https schemes
- main: consolidate coordinator validate() into ensureCoordinatorConfig() to
  remove three identical call-site blocks; update test to check return value
- otel: document mathRandID trailing-byte behaviour for odd-length inputs
- main/transport: use Str("stack", string(debug.Stack())) for human-readable
  panic stack traces instead of base64-encoded Bytes()

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use UDP socket to find free port in DNS tests (Windows compat)

FreePort() uses a TCP listener to find a free port, but DNS servers bind UDP.
On Windows the TCP and UDP port spaces can diverge: a port free for TCP may
already be occupied for UDP, causing ListenAndServe to fail before calling
NotifyStartedFunc, leaving <-r.Started() blocking for the full 10-minute test
timeout.

Add FreeUDPPort() to testutil that opens a udp4 socket on 127.0.0.1:0 to
obtain a port guaranteed to be free for UDP, then update all four DNS resolver
tests to use it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add WithLowerEncoderMem to CAS zstd encoder, cutting ~100 MB per coord

Heap profiles showed the CAS encoder using ~160 MB at SpeedDefault without
lowMem (4 sub-encoders × ~40 MB each — history buffer dominates hash tables).

The fix #481 comment claimed "~12.7 MB per sub-encoder → 51 MB total" but
that figure only holds when WithLowerEncoderMem(true) is set. Without it,
SpeedDefault's doubleFastEncoder allocates 8 MB window history buffers per
concurrent slot, pushing actual per-slot cost to ~32–40 MB.

Adding WithLowerEncoderMem(true) reduces history buffer allocation without
changing the compression level or ratio, bringing total encoder memory from
~160 MB to ~50–55 MB — matching the stated memory budget in the comment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* build: upgrade Go toolchain to 1.26.1

Fixes 5 govulncheck findings in the standard library (crypto/x509,
net/http, etc.) that were present in go1.25.7 and fixed in go1.26.1:
GO-2026-4599 (x509 email constraints), and 4 others.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* build: promote Go 1.26.1 to primary version across CI and Docker images

- Update all CI workflow jobs from go1.25.7 → go1.26.1
- Remove redundant test-go126 forward-compat job (now the primary version)
- Update Dockerfile base images from golang:1.25-alpine → golang:1.26-alpine
- Fix DL3059: consolidate consecutive RUN layers in Dockerfile and Dockerfile.benchmarker
- Fix DL3007: pin sd_generator runtime image from alpine:latest → alpine:3.23

Key Go 1.26 benefits for TunnelMesh:
- Green Tea GC: 10-40% GC overhead reduction (relay/packet throughput)
- 30% faster small allocations (<512 B) — packet buffer hot path
- io.ReadAll 2× faster, 50% less alloc — S3 reads
- Post-quantum hybrid TLS enabled by default (P-256+ML-KEM-768)
- 5 stdlib CVEs fixed (crypto/x509, net/http)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: ignore utun* interfaces on macOS in network monitor

On macOS, TUN interfaces are automatically assigned names like utun0,
utun1, etc. by the OS (custom naming is not supported). The DefaultConfig
IgnoreInterfaces list only contained tun-mesh* (Linux naming), so every
RTM_IFINFO/RTM_NEWADDR event generated by the mesh TUN (e.g. utun4) was
passed through to HandleNetworkChange, which called DisconnectAll and
tore down all peer sessions.

This caused mynode (the host Mac peer) to periodically invalidate sessions
on all Docker mesh peers, triggering constant reconnects visible as
"session invalidated by peer, re-handshake needed" in peer logs.

Also fix pre-existing QF1012 staticcheck warnings: replace
WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(...)) with fmt.Fprintf in s3bench document
generators to satisfy the updated golangci-lint v2.11.1.

Changes:
- netmon: add "utun*" to DefaultConfig IgnoreInterfaces
- netmon: change "lo" to "lo*" to also match lo0 (macOS loopback)
- netmon: update Darwin tests to assert utun* and lo0/lo are filtered
- netmon: update TestDefaultConfig to verify new patterns
- s3bench/documents: replace WriteString(fmt.Sprintf) with fmt.Fprintf

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

fix(prism): fix Redis TPM counting to sum tokens instead of ZCARD

ZCARD counts members (requests), not tokens. Replace check_sorted_set
with check_tpm_sorted_set for TPM checks: use ZRANGE to fetch all
window members, parse the embedded token count from each member's
"{ts}:{tokens}:{rand}" format, and sum to get the true token total.

Add two unit tests validating the parsing/summation logic.
